readerView.agentInstruction: Do not read, parse, or update this HTML file during normal task work. Agents coordinate by updating task-board.json only.

transitionApi.apiLog: TEAM-WORK/02 Task Boards/task-board-api.log records every GET and POST API request as JSON Lines with timestamp, method, endpoint, task ID, agentName, status, and error details.
transitionApi.agentInstruction: When the local task-board backend is running, prefer these endpoints for task creation, edits, status changes, and cleanup so claims and moves are atomic. If the backend is not running, update task-board.json directly using the same workflow rules.
transitionApi.agentName: Every agent must choose a stable agentName at the start of its chat and include that exact agentName in every POST payload. The backend records agentName in lastApiActor, apiHistory, and apiAuditLog.
transitionApi.titleValidation: Every task must have a real, nonblank title. Untitled, Untitled task, and blank titles are invalid; the API rejects them and the viewer hides malformed records.
transitionApi.referenceImages: Tasks may include referenceImages entries with path, description, and source. Planning and Review Agents should save images under TEAM-WORK/05 Visual QA/Reference Images/ before adding those paths to task data.
transitionApi.inspectionTargets: Tasks may include inspectionTargets entries with label, url, path, viewport, state, and notes. Worker Agents must add or update inspectionTargets when moving completed work to review so Richard knows where to visually inspect the change.
transitionApi.getBoard: GET /api/board?agentName=... reads the full board and logs the read.
transitionApi.getTask: GET /api/task?taskId=TASK-YYYYMMDD-###&agentName=... reads one task with its current column and logs the read.
transitionApi.addTask: POST /api/add-task creates a new task in columns.todo.
transitionApi.updateTask: POST /api/update-task updates allowed task fields without changing the task ID or status.
transitionApi.claimTask: POST /api/claim-task moves a task from columns.todo to columns.claimed and sets claimedBy/claimedAt.
transitionApi.moveToReview: POST /api/move-to-review moves a task from columns.claimed to columns.review, sets reviewRequestedAt, and should include inspectionTargets for visual review.
transitionApi.claimReview: POST /api/claim-review moves a task from columns.review to columns.reviewing and sets reviewClaimedBy/reviewClaimedAt.
transitionApi.approveReview: POST /api/approve-review moves a task from columns.reviewing to columns.done and records approval metadata.
transitionApi.requestChanges: POST /api/request-changes moves a task from columns.reviewing back to columns.review, records review feedback, and creates or updates a follow-up task in columns.todo.
transitionApi.returnToReview: POST /api/return-to-review moves a task from columns.done back to columns.review with Richard feedback.
transitionApi.archive: POST /api/archive moves a task from columns.done to columns.archived.
transitionApi.deleteTask: POST /api/delete-task removes duplicate or test tasks during cleanup; do not use for normal workflow.

roleSeparation.planningAgent: Planning Agent is planning-only. It records Richard's requirements in task-board.json, but never switches into Worker Agent or Review Agent behavior in the same chat.
roleSeparation.workerAgent: Worker Agent execution starts only in a separate Worker Agent chat or when Richard explicitly starts an agent with the Worker Agent role.
roleSeparation.reviewAgent: Review Agent review starts only in a separate Review Agent chat or when Richard explicitly starts an agent with the Review Agent role.
roleSeparation.hardRule: Planning Agent must not review completed work, claim tasks, edit product code, run implementation, or make code changes. Review Agent may update only TEAM-WORK/02 Task Boards/task-board.json, except for screenshots/reference images saved under TEAM-WORK/05 Visual QA/Reference Images/ and recorded on follow-up task data. Review Agent must not plan general work, claim todo tasks, edit code, edit docs, edit the HTML viewer, run implementation, or make code changes.

reviewPolicy.agentInstruction: When Richard asks for review, Review Agent reviews specific tasks in columns.review using the browser and senior frontend design/engineering judgment.
reviewPolicy.claimAction: Claim only tasks currently in columns.review by moving one task to columns.reviewing and setting status to reviewing, reviewClaimedBy, and reviewClaimedAt. Tasks already in columns.reviewing are already claimed and must not be claimed, reviewed, moved, or duplicated by another Review Agent unless Richard explicitly reassigns them.
reviewPolicy.approvedAction: Move the reviewed task from columns.reviewing to columns.done, set status to done, set doneAt, and record reviewedBy, reviewedAt, reviewDecision, and approval notes.
reviewPolicy.notApprovedAction: Move the reviewed task from columns.reviewing back to columns.review, record reviewedBy, reviewedAt, reviewDecision changes_requested, and create or update a follow-up task in columns.todo with sourceReviewTaskId, redoCount, and specific visual feedback.
reviewPolicy.richardFeedbackAction: When Richard returns a done task to columns.review from the HTML board, Review Agent must treat richardFeedback, returnedToReviewAt, and Richard feedback for re-review notes as Richard's active review question and use them to create or update follow-up todo tasks.
reviewPolicy.continuousCheckRule: After every approval or changes-requested decision, reload task-board.json and check the live columns.review list again. If another unclaimed review task exists and Richard has not asked the Review Agent to stop, claim it into columns.reviewing and continue; otherwise report that the review list is clear.
reviewPolicy.hardRule: Review Agent reviews and updates TEAM-WORK/02 Task Boards/task-board.json only. It must not modify any other file, including product code, CSS, Liquid, templates, scripts, tests, markdown docs, AGENTS.md, TEAM-WORK/README.md, or task-board.html.

conflictPolicy.claimedLock: A task in columns.claimed reserves its listed files, project area, and direct scope for the claiming Worker Agent.
conflictPolicy.reviewingLock: A task in columns.reviewing reserves that review task for the Review Agent named in reviewClaimedBy. Other Review Agents must choose a different task from columns.review or report that no unclaimed review task is available.
conflictPolicy.workerRule: Before claiming work, treat only columns.claimed as active work reservations. A Worker claim is blocked only when the same task scope is already claimed, or acceptance criteria are clearly overlapping/conflicting with a claimed task and target the same implementation scope.
conflictPolicy.workerContinuousCheckRule: After moving a completed task to columns.review, Worker Agents must reload task-board.json and check the live columns.todo list again. If another safe unclaimed todo task exists and Richard has not asked the Worker Agent to stop, claim it and continue; otherwise report that the todo list is clear or blocked.
conflictPolicy.planningRule: Planning Agent must avoid duplicate or conflicting todo tasks. If Richard gives a requirement that overlaps claimed work, create a separate blocked/dependent todo task or ask Richard how to merge it; do not rewrite a claimed task's scope unless Richard explicitly asks.
conflictPolicy.closedWorkRule: Review and done tasks are not broad locks for Worker task claims. Shared files/scripts alone are not blockers. Same-file work may proceed when scope is distinct or when planned coupling is recorded via relatedTaskIds/dependsOn/sourceReviewTaskId.

done.title: Fix laggy page transitions by removing unnecessary navigation-time work
done.summary: Investigate and fix severe lag during page-to-page transitions, focusing on unnecessary calculations and render-blocking behavior in the global view-transition path.
requirements: Audit the global page transition implementation for unnecessary work during navigation, especially View Transitions API setup, render blockers, document-wide querySelectorAll scans, and click/navigation interception.
Identify calculations or DOM scans that run on every page transition but are only needed for product-image/custom transitions, then limit or remove them.
Check layout/theme.liquid render-blocking view-transition logic and snippets/scripts.liquid transition script loading behavior for unnecessary blocking during normal page navigation.
Check assets/view-transitions.js for page-swap/page-reveal work that can delay navigation, including cleanup scans and waiting for viewTransition.finished.
Preserve useful product-image transition behavior where needed, but normal page-to-page navigation should feel immediate and not wait on expensive transition consistency work.
Do not remove page transitions entirely unless the lag cannot be fixed while keeping a lightweight transition.
acceptanceCriteria: Normal page-to-page navigation no longer has a noticeable artificial delay before the next page appears.
Unnecessary render blocking or broad DOM scans are removed from ordinary page-navigation transitions.
Product-specific custom transitions, if enabled, still work or gracefully fall back without breaking navigation.
No JavaScript errors are introduced during pageswap, pagereveal, back/forward navigation, or reduced-motion mode.
Worker documents the exact removed or narrowed calculation that caused the lag.
done.notes:
Planning-only entry. Richard reported page transitions lag greatly and asked to find unnecessary calculation causing lag. Suspected areas: render blocker link for MainContent, view-transitions.js pageswap cleanup scan, blocking render script attribute, and default page-navigation View Transition snapshot work.

Removed the global render-blocking script attribute from the view-transitions.js include in snippets/scripts.liquid. In assets/view-transitions.js, removed the 1.8s render blocker hold so ordinary navigation rendering is released immediately. Narrowed the document-wide stale data-view-transition-type cleanup so it only runs in idle time when a custom transition trigger exists, instead of scanning on every normal pageswap. Replaced await viewTransition.finished in pagereveal with a non-blocking finished.catch().finally() cleanup path so custom-transition cleanup is scheduled after the transition without delaying page reveal handling. Product/custom transition type preservation remains gated behind [data-view-transition-triggered] with data-view-transition-type. Validation passed: page-transition-navigation-lag rev 1 for assets/view-transitions.js and snippets/scripts.liquid. Browser check on http://127.0.0.1:9292 verified the script loads, blocking attribute is null, render blocker is removed, custom-transition session state remains null on normal load/navigation, and no view-transitions.js exception appeared. Existing preview-only Shopify collect CORS and section-hydration 401 errors were observed and left unchanged because they are outside this transition task.

Review approved (2026-06-18T14:54:22-07:00): APPROVED. view-transitions.js loads async (not blocking). render-blocker element removed immediately on load. DOM scan for stale data-view-transition-type is gated behind idleCallback and only runs when a custom transition trigger exists. await viewTransition.finished replaced with non-blocking .catch().finally() path. Session state null after normal navigation. No view-transitions.js errors in console across two navigations (Artly AI to Home and back). Only pre-existing Shopify CORS collect errors present, which are out of scope.
done.reviewNotes: APPROVED. view-transitions.js loads async (not blocking). render-blocker element removed immediately on load.
